linkedin:
**Most people discover their first "hidden" subscription charge within 60 seconds of uploading a bank statement.**

One user found Netflix had auto-upgraded them to Premium ($19.99/mo instead of $6.99). They never clicked "Upgrade." Happened 8 months ago. That's $104 they didn't know was leaving.

Another uploaded their last quarter's statements. Spotify was still charging $11.99/mo. They'd switched to Apple Music in 2024 but forgot to cancel. Three other apps—gym membership, podcast app, cloud storage—all quietly running.

Total damage: $340/year. Found in 90 seconds.

This isn't rare. It's the default.

Americans spend $219/month on subscriptions they think they're using. 59.9% admit they have paid subscriptions they never touch. By Friday, most people will have forgotten about half the apps they pay for.

The question isn't whether you have hidden charges.

It's whether you'll find them before next month's bill.

**What will YOU uncover? No login required. Privacy-first. Takes 2 minutes.**

[**leakly-psi.vercel.app**](https://leakly-psi.vercel.app)

#HiddenCharges #PersonalFinance #SubscriptionAudit
